at all levels.explanation_en: including people from every position or rank in an organization
at all levels.example_sentence: The company held meetings with employees at all levels to hear their concerns.
at all levels.original_sentence: In hundreds of interviews with people at all levels, I've made this discovery.

apt.explanation_en: likely to do something or having a natural tendency
apt.example_sentence: He is apt to forget his keys when he is in a hurry.
apt.original_sentence: The bigger the person, the more apt he is to encourage you to talk.

preach.explanation_en: to give moral advice in an annoying or bossy way
preach.example_sentence: Don't preach to me about my lifestyle; I know what I'm doing.
preach.original_sentence: The smaller the person, the more apt he is to preach to you.

monopolize.explanation_en: to take control of something so that others cannot use or share it
monopolize.example_sentence: He tends to monopolize the conversation at dinner parties.

in all walks of life.explanation_en: in all different types of jobs and social positions
in all walks of life.example_sentence: People in all walks of life enjoy playing football.
in all walks of life.original_sentence: Top-level leaders in all walks of life spend much more time requesting advice than they do in giving it.

under these circumstances.example_sentence: Under these circumstances, it is best to wait for more information.
under these circumstances.original_sentence: What would you do under these circumstances?

raw material.explanation_en: basic substances used to make something else
raw material.example_sentence: Wood is the raw material used to make this table.
raw material.original_sentence: Now, to manufacture anything, you've got to have raw material.

ready-made.explanation_en: prepared in advance and ready to be used immediately
ready-made.example_sentence: There are no ready-made solutions to this complex problem.
ready-made.original_sentence: Don't, of course, expect other people to give you ready-made solutions.

spark.explanation_en: to trigger or start a process or idea
spark.example_sentence: The book helped to spark my interest in history.
spark.original_sentence: Ideas of others help to spark your own ideas, so your mind is more creative.

highlight.explanation_en: the best or most interesting part of an event
highlight.example_sentence: The highlight of the trip was visiting the ancient castle.
highlight.original_sentence: One of the highlights of each meeting was a 15-minute discussion by one of the executives on the topic.

pressing.explanation_en: needing to be dealt with immediately; urgent
pressing.example_sentence: Climate change is one of the most pressing issues of our time.
pressing.original_sentence: How I Solved My Most Pressing Management Problem.

outlined.explanation_en: to give a summary or the main points of something
outlined.example_sentence: The manager outlined the new project goals in the meeting.
outlined.original_sentence: He quickly outlined his problem and then asked the group for ideas on solving it.

to be sure.example_sentence: To be sure, it was a difficult task, but we finished it on time.
to be sure.original_sentence: To be sure, he got a record of each idea suggested.

compliment.explanation_en: to tell someone that you admire something they have done
compliment.example_sentence: She complimented him on his excellent presentation.
compliment.original_sentence: Later I talked with this man and complimented him on his unique approach.

sharp.explanation_en: intelligent and quick to understand things
sharp.example_sentence: He is a very sharp student who learns quickly.
sharp.original_sentence: His comment was, there are some very sharp men in this group.

harvest.explanation_en: to collect or gather information or ideas
harvest.example_sentence: We hope to harvest some useful feedback from the survey.
harvest.original_sentence: I just figured I'd harvest some ideas.

clue.explanation_en: a piece of information that helps solve a problem or mystery
clue.example_sentence: The detective found a clue that helped solve the case.
clue.original_sentence: There's a good possibility something someone said during that session may give me the clue I need to solve the problem.

in so doing.example_sentence: He exercised every day; in so doing, he improved his health significantly.
in so doing.original_sentence: In so doing, he got some decision-making raw material, and as a side benefit, the other executives in the audience enjoyed the discussion.

side benefit.explanation_en: an extra advantage that happens while doing something else
side benefit.example_sentence: A side benefit of walking to work is that I save money on gas.
side benefit.original_sentence: In so doing, he got some decision-making raw material, and as a side benefit, the other executives in the audience enjoyed the discussion.

take part.explanation_en: to participate or be involved in an activity
take part.example_sentence: Everyone is encouraged to take part in the school play.
take part.original_sentence: It gave them the opportunity to take part.
